refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain end-to-end teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to ensure safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly include the latency metric and network conditions clarifies the performance requirement and safety context.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of teleoperation communication under defined network conditions during system integration testing and validate it meets ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication processing delay within the NGHE solution.
FR2: Ensure reliable and timely data transmission over the network link.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with low latency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize communication protocol stack for low latency.
DP2: Use dedicated high-speed network link with QoS guarantees.
DP3: Implement edge processing to reduce data volume and processing time.
DP4: Prioritize teleoperation data packets in network scheduling.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Optimize communication protocol stack for low latency.

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on communication protocol optimization, which logically maps to the teleoperation function with minimal coupling to unrelated functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed parameters for latency measurement.
Risk that protocol optimization alone may not achieve latency target if network conditions degrade.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating network QoS mechanisms (DP2) if latency targets are not met by protocol optimization alone.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication processing and protocol handling.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time telemetry data to other modules and stakeholders.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and tasking instructions.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment score as the DP maps directly to Teleoperation module; low diffusion indicating focused responsibility.
Broader module with mixed responsibilities; less ideal for containment of latency optimization.
Minimal containment relevance; vehicle module is physical hardware focused.
Safety services have indirect involvement; not primary containment for latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP to Teleoperation module centralizes latency optimization responsibility, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network link quality outside module control.
Risk of interface latency not fully captured if other modules introduce delays.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor cross-module latency contributions and consider interface performance contracts.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear mapping and no excessive coupling.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk due to focused containment in Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency on the Teleoperation module interfaces under test conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Consider adding network QoS mechanisms if latency targets are not met.
Define interface performance contracts to manage cross-module latency.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition is sufficient and consistent with requirements, design parameters, and implementation modules. No immediate need to zigzag or revise at this level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication processing delay within the NGHE solution.
FR2: Ensure reliable and timely data transmission over the network link.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with low latency.

revise.why: No significant issues detected requiring revision at this level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement protocol optimizations effectively.
open_risks: Cross-module latency contributions not fully controlled.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement reports under test network conditions.
Interface performance data between Teleoperation and other modules.
